evidence: `src/domains/generation/types/generationRow.ts` and `src/domains/generation/types/shot.ts` each merge persistence DTO fields with UI/view-model fields in a single exported type (`GenerationRow`, `Shot`), then expose conversion helpers that flatten both concerns.

`src/integrations/supabase/client.ts` keeps module-level singleton state (`clientInstance`, `instrumentationInstalled`) and performs runtime bootstrap/instrumentation implicitly via accessor calls, creating hidden global lifecycle coupling.
`src/shared/components/ui/sonner.tsx` pulls a shared toast manager via `getToastManager()` inside a UI primitive wrapper, indicating cross-cutting global state ownership living in generic UI surface.

findings.summary: Core generation/shot types conflate DB contracts and UI state in shared domain surfaces.
evidence: `GenerationRow` extends both `PersistedGenerationRow` and `GenerationViewModel` in one exported type used broadly.
`Shot` extends both `PersistedShotRow` and `ShotViewModel`, and `toShot`/`toGenerationRow` flatten both layers into one object shape.
findings.suggestion: Split persistence and UI models into separate modules (for example `.../persistence/*` and `.../view/*`), keep conversion at feature boundaries, and stop exporting merged row types as the default cross-module contract.

findings.summary: Shared entrypoints own hidden global runtime state, making ownership and lifecycle boundaries implicit.
evidence: `client.ts` stores singleton flags/instance at module scope and performs instrumentation/bootstrap through `getSupabaseClient()` side effects.
`sonner.tsx` fetches a global toast manager from `getToastManager()` inside a generic `ui` wrapper, coupling UI rendering to app-wide singleton state.
`useLightboxViewportLock.ts` mutates global document/window state (`html.classList`, global focusout listener, `window.scrollTo`) from a hook without a centralized viewport lock service.
findings.suggestion: Introduce explicit runtime providers/services (e.g., `SupabaseRuntimeProvider`, `ToastRuntimeProvider`, `ViewportLockService`) and move singleton/global mutations behind app bootstrap boundaries so shared UI/hooks consume injected contracts instead of owning global lifecycle.

findings.summary: `shared/components` mixes auth gating, runtime recovery, and generic UI concerns.
evidence: `AuthGate` directly imports `useAuth` from auth context, making it an auth-policy boundary component.
`ChunkLoadErrorBoundary` handles lazy chunk deployment mismatch and page reload behavior (runtime/platform concern).
`ConfirmDialog` is a reusable dialog abstraction over `AlertDialog` UI primitives, a different ownership layer.
findings.suggestion: Split `shared/components` by ownership boundary: move `AuthGate` under an auth-oriented package (for example `src/shared/auth/` or a domain auth package), move `ChunkLoadErrorBoundary` under app/runtime infrastructure (for example `src/app/errors/`), and keep `ConfirmDialog` under UI/shared component space.

findings.summary: `shared/hooks` is a flattened bucket containing unrelated interaction and preference concerns.
evidence: `useClickRipple` is pointer-event animation behavior tied to visual interaction state.
`useCopyToClipboard` is clipboard side-effect handling with UI feedback timing.
`useDarkMode` is theme preference persistence on top of persistent state storage.
All three live at the same package depth and test bucket, so directory structure does not communicate ownership/change boundaries.
findings.suggestion: Introduce concern-based hook subpackages (for example `shared/hooks/interaction/`, `shared/hooks/clipboard/`, `shared/hooks/theme/`) and mirror tests per subpackage so package boundaries describe who owns and changes each concern.
